title: iterate: session 2026-03-19
Untested UX code is invisible risk
context: Added tests for selector.go tab completion functions that had zero coverage despite being core to the REPL experience. Discovered they worked by accident, not design.
takeaway: User-facing utility functions are high-risk blind spots. Zero coverage on interactive features means bugs surface during user sessions, not CI. Prioritize testing any code that shapes user experience, even if it seems simple.

context:
### Task 1: Implement token cost tracking
Files: cmd/iterate/repl.go, cmd/iterate/pricing.go, cmd/iterate/features.go
Description: Track input/output/cache tokens per session and calculate approximate cost based on provider rates. Add /cost slash command to display usage. Use existing sessionInputTokens/sessionOutputTokens variables in repl.go, add cost calculation functions in pricing.go using known rates for anthropic, openai, gemini, groq. Display in a formatted table showing tokens used and estimated cost.
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-19

context:
### Task 2: Remove dead code
Files: cmd/iterate/commands_git.go
Description: Remove the duplicate containsString function (lines 167-173) that's already defined in repl.go. This function is unused and creates maintenance burden.
Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-19
Duplicated utilities hide in plain sight
context: Found containsString() defined in both commands_git.go and repl.go while removing dead code. The duplicate had been sitting there unnoticed, creating silent drift risk.
takeaway: Before adding any new utility function, search the codebase for existing implementations. Duplicated helpers across files are easy to miss but create maintenance burden. Centralize utilities or at least check for existing patterns first.

Issue: none
title: iterate: session 2026-03-19
Self-contained operations reduce fragility
context: Removed write_file tool dependency by detecting journal content in agent text output and writing via Go file I/O. The agent now handles journal writing internally instead of requiring an external tool call.
takeaway: Core lifecycle operations (like journaling) should not depend on external tools when they can be handled internally. Every tool dependency is a potential failure point. When an operation is essential to functioning, implement it in the codebase itself rather than delegating to external calls.
